description: Rules for viewport utility density - ensuring pages maximize functional content above the fold

rules.description: Every page-level asset MUST be classified as WEBAPP, DOCUMENTATION, PRODUCT, or MARKETING before layout begins

action.message: Classify page type before layout: WEBAPP (task completion), DOCUMENTATION (find & understand), PRODUCT (evaluate & convert), MARKETING (inspire & convert). Each type has different VUD targets and hero allowances.

rules.rationale: Page type determines viewport allocation strategy. WEBAPP and DOCUMENTATION must never waste viewport on hero images. PRODUCT and MARKETING have controlled allowances.

rules.description: WEBAPP and DOCUMENTATION pages must NEVER have hero images or decorative full-width headers

action.target: hero images and decorative headers on webapp/docs pages
action.message: WEBAPP and DOCUMENTATION pages must start with functional content immediately. No hero images, no decorative gradients, no oversized headings (max text-2xl for page title). Every pixel above the fold must serve a user task.

rules.rationale: Hero images and decorative headers waste 40%+ of viewport on webapp/docs pages. Users come to these pages to DO something or FIND something, not to be inspired.

examples.correct: 56px header bar → 48px toolbar with filters → data table filling remaining viewport
examples.incorrect: 400px hero with gradient and 72px 'Welcome to your Dashboard' heading

examples.correct: 48px header with search → TOC sidebar + content body starting at line 1
examples.incorrect: 200px hero with product logo and tagline before any docs content

rules.description: Page layouts MUST meet minimum Viewport Utility Density (VUD) score for their page type

action.message: Calculate VUD: Σ(element_area × weight) / ATF_area. Targets: WEBAPP ≥ 0.70, DOCUMENTATION ≥ 0.75, PRODUCT ≥ 0.50, MARKETING ≥ 0.35. Weights: primary task UI = 1.0, navigation = 0.8, search = 0.9, decorative = 0.05, wasted = 0.0

rules.rationale: VUD ensures layouts maximize functional content. A score below target means too much viewport is consumed by decoration, whitespace, or non-functional elements.

rules.description: Marketing page heroes MUST not exceed 60vh and MUST contain a visible CTA and value proposition

action.message: Marketing heroes: max 60vh height, MUST include visible CTA button and clear value proposition. Product heroes: max 40vh, must include key features or specs. Hero without CTA or value prop is pure decoration (VUD weight 0.05).

rules.rationale: Even marketing pages should not waste entire viewport on decoration. The hero must communicate value and provide a clear action path, not just look pretty.

examples.correct: Hero at 55vh with headline, 2-line value prop, CTA button, and a product screenshot showing the actual UI
examples.incorrect: Hero at 100vh with abstract gradient, company name in 96px font, and 'Learn More' link below the fold

rules.description: All P1 (primary priority) content MUST be fully visible above the fold without scrolling

action.message: P1 content (primary user tasks, key data, main navigation) must be fully visible within the initial viewport (above the fold). Use viewport height budget: webapp header 56px + toolbar 48px + primary content 600px+ on a 768px effective height.

rules.rationale: Users should not need to scroll to find their primary tasks. The initial viewport is the most valuable real estate and must be reserved for highest-priority content.
